items.title: Flies ‘change their tune’ according to female behaviour during courtship
items.author: Sian Lewis
items.content: Perturbing female Drosophila behaviour during courtship induces adaptive changes in innate male fly courtship song patterns; these changes are initially retained in subsequent courtship encounters with normal female behaviour, but songs gradually return to innate patterns.

items.title: Opportunities and pitfalls of data contextualization in neuroimaging
items.author: Boris C. Bernhardt
items.content: Despite rapid exploitation of the opportunities that contextualization of brain maps affords, potential limitations have received little attention. In this Roadmap, Royer et al. provide practical guidelines operating at the level of study design, analysis pipelines and interpretation of findings to encourage the development of best practices in data contextualization in neuroscience.

items.title: Reply to ‘Musical neurodynamics and the ‘inner voice’’
items.author: Caroline Palmer
items.content: Reply to ‘Musical neurodynamics and the ‘inner voice’’

items.title: Neurogenic signatures of cognitive decline and resilience
items.author: Katherine Whalley
items.content: Single-nucleus sequencing reveals age- and disease-related changes in the molecular regulation of neurogenesis associated with cognitive decline and resilience.

items.title: Role of dysregulated calcium homeostasis in astrocytes in neurodegenerative disorders
items.author: Brian J. Bacskai
items.content: Astrocytic Ca2+ signalling is a fundamental and highly spatiotemporally nuanced mechanism for maintaining brain homeostasis. In this Review, Bacskai and colleagues discuss how astrocytic Ca2+ homeostasis is altered in ageing and neurodegenerative diseases and plays a central role in neurodegenerative pathophysiology.

items.title: Clarifying the conceptual dimensions of representation in neuroscience
items.author: Wei Ji Ma
items.content: Appeals to representation are widespread, despite neuroscientists’ uncertainty about what kind of findings count as evidence for such claims. In this Perspective, Pohl and colleagues develop a unified framework that distinguishes four conceptual dimensions relevant to representation, illustrating them in information-theoretic terms to explicitly characterize representation in neuroscience.

items.title: Regulating intestinal immunity
items.author: Darran Yates
items.content: A new study in mice reveals a neuron–epithelial cell circuit in the intestine that promotes type 2 immunity.
